I respectfully disagree. Jensen himself admits that the odds of agents building a company of Nvidia's complexity are 0%. If a system cannot replicate the multi-disciplinary, long-term strategic reasoning required to build a complex hardware-software ecosystem like Nvidia, it suggests we are still in the era of "Narrow" or "Task-Oriented" AI, rather than "General" AI.

I love how David talks about building a bunch of prototypes and that in the long run you make more progress. That is why SpaceX is so successful.  There first Starship prototype was thought to be a water tower.  Arguments went on for weeks until one day is was clear to everyone it was a rocket.  SpaceX then went about building and blowing up a lot of prototypes and now Starship is finally building block 3 which will be production.     Wish the best to David and his team and keep those prototypes going until you're finally ready to build a working generator that can power a datacenter.  That will be very cool to see.

1:03:35 "We're all infantile..." because our culture (especially Western/American culture) does not have mechanisms or expectations that actually move the majority of people to grow into truly Mature human beings. Most of our ideas about "human nature" are based on our observations of immature humans, and thinking, "That's as good as it gets." This becomes more obvious if you have had reason or opportunity to mature beyond that "average" level of human maturity and functioning. Also, this is not just about practical, functional maturing, it's also about spiritual maturing. Christianity tends to encourage a "child-parent" relationship with the Divine and seeking for "unconditional (no expectation) love." In religions that see God as a "guru" or "guruing force" and the individual as a devotee, not just a believer, there are far more demands for self-awareness, self-discipline, and growth, or higher and higher levels of realization or understanding, including practical and spiritual maturing.
